← All worlds/World 05
World 05 · 1 state

Self-Worth

Films for the long argument with the voice in your head — the one that says you’re not enough, and the slow process of stopping believing it.

Choose an emotion
Feeling Like You’re Not Enough
On Feeling Like You’re Not Enough

Nobody told you where the bar was. They just expected you to clear it. And somehow you internalised a standard so exacting that nothing you do quite reaches it — not the promotion, not the relationship, not the year you finally thought you had it together. This is the quiet violence of not-enoughness: it moves the target. These films do not offer affirmations. They offer something more useful — the company of people who were measured against impossible standards and found ways to live that were honest instead of compliant.

Feeling Like You’re Not Enough

Films for the voice that keeps saying you’re behind.

CS9.1
1997

Good Will Hunting

Gus Van Sant

View Film →

A self-taught janitor at MIT, secretly a mathematical prodigy, is taken on by a math professor and a therapist who refuse to let him sabotage his future.

CS9.0
1976

Rocky

John G. Avildsen

View Film →

A small-time Philadelphia club fighter is plucked from obscurity to face the heavyweight champion of the world. The film is about whether he can stay standing.

CS8.3
2006

The Pursuit of Happyness

Gabriele Muccino

View Film →

A struggling salesman in 1980s San Francisco loses his apartment and competes for a single unpaid spot in a stockbroker programme while caring for his young son.

CS9.0
2020

Soul

Pete Docter

View Film →

A middle-school music teacher who finally lands the gig of his dreams dies on the same day — and finds himself in a celestial waystation, trying to get back.

CS8.7
2006

Little Miss Sunshine

Dayton & Faris

View Film →

A dysfunctional family piles into a yellow VW van to drive their seven-year-old daughter from Albuquerque to a Californian beauty pageant.

CS8.8
1989

Dead Poets Society

Peter Weir

View Film →

An English teacher at an elite 1950s boarding school inspires his students to seize their lives through poetry — with consequences nobody is ready for.

CS7.9
2013

The Secret Life of Walter Mitty

Ben Stiller

View Film →

A daydreaming photo archivist at LIFE magazine, faced with losing his job, finally steps outside his fantasies and chases down one missing negative across the world.

CS8.6
2012

Silver Linings Playbook

David O. Russell

View Film →

A man newly out of a psychiatric facility moves back in with his parents and meets a young widow with her own grief — the two become each other’s argument for a life.

CS8.4
2019

The Peanut Butter Falcon

Nilson & Schwartz

View Film →

A young man with Down syndrome runs away from his care facility to chase his dream of becoming a pro wrestler, and finds an unlikely friend on the way.

CS8.2
2014

Chef

Jon Favreau

View Film →

A high-end LA chef whose career is sabotaged by a critic and a restaurant owner buys a food truck with his son and slowly remembers why he started cooking.

CS9.2
2014

Whiplash

Damien Chazelle

View Film →

A first-year drummer at a competitive music academy is taken on by the school’s most feared instructor. The cost of becoming great.

CS8.8
2000

Billy Elliot

Stephen Daldry

View Film →

An eleven-year-old boy in a mining town during the 1984 UK strike discovers ballet and fights for the right to pursue it.

CS8.4
2018

Capernaum

Nadine Labaki

View Film →

A twelve-year-old boy in Beirut sues his parents for bringing him into the world. The film is his testimony about what that world looked like.

CS8.8
2015

Creed

Ryan Coogler

View Film →

The son of Apollo Creed trains under Rocky Balboa to earn his own name as a boxer.

CS8.3
2019

Rocketman

Dexter Fletcher

View Film →

Elton John’s life from Pinner childhood to superstardom, told as a fantasy musical where the songs are his inner life.

CS8.4
2016

Hidden Figures

Theodore Melfi

View Film →

Three Black women mathematicians at NASA calculate the trajectories for the early US space program in 1960s Virginia.

CS8.3
2016

Captain Fantastic

Matt Ross

View Film →

A father raises his six children off-grid in the Pacific Northwest forest. The outside world eventually forces itself in.

CS8.3
1999

October Sky

Joe Johnston

View Film →

A coal miner’s son in 1957 West Virginia decides to build rockets after Sputnik launches, fighting his father’s expectations and the town’s limitations.

CS8.5
2016

I, Daniel Blake

Ken Loach

View Film →

A carpenter recovering from a heart attack in Newcastle is denied welfare benefits and navigates a system designed to exhaust him.

CS9.1
2018

Minding the Gap

Bing Liu

View Film →

A documentary director follows two childhood friends in Rockford, Illinois through their twenties — skateboarding, fatherhood, violence, and escape.

CS7.8
2015

Eddie the Eagle

Dexter Fletcher

View Film →

The true story of Michael Edwards, the first British Olympic ski jumper, who finished last in the 1988 Calgary Games and became beloved.

CS8.3
2006

The Pursuit of Happyness

Gabriele Muccino

View Film →

A struggling salesman in 1980s San Francisco loses his apartment and competes for a single unpaid internship while caring for his young son.

CS8.1
1993

Rudy

David Anspaugh

View Film →

A young man from a steel-town family dreams of playing football for Notre Dame despite no athletic gifts. He tries for four years.

CS8.0
2017

Gifted

Marc Webb

View Film →

A Florida man raises his mathematically gifted seven-year-old niece and fights his mother’s desire to send her to a special school.

Purpose & Direction →

For the dream that won’t let go.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
